standards.description: Assess the reasonableness of a solution in its given context, including financial literacy applications. Compare the solution to appropriate graphical or numerical estimates. Interpret a solution in the original context. (MP3) $ ☼

standards.description: Use the structure of an expression, equation and/or formula to create an equivalent form that is more helpful given the situation. Rearrange formulas to highlight a quantity of interest, using the same reasoning in solving equations. (MP6) $
standards.topic: Number Relationships: Describe, Interpret and use quantities, relationships between quantities, representations of quantities, and number systems. standards.description: Use the structure of an expression to write it in multiple ways. (MP7)

standards.description: Add, subtract and multiply polynomials. (MP1, MP7)
standards.topic: Equivalence and Relational Thinking: Use concepts and properties of equivalence and relational thinking to represent and compare numerical expressions, proportional relationships, algebraic expressions and equations.

standards.description: Reason abstractly to compare general forms of quadratics, including vertex form, general form, factored form and the graph. Develop procedures to convert from one form to another. (MP2, MP7) μ
standards.topic: Equivalence and Relational Thinking: Use concepts and properties of equivalence and relational thinking to represent and compare numerical expressions, proportional relationships, algebraic expressions and equations.

standards.description: Choose and produce an equivalent form of a quadratic function, using symbolic and graphical methods, to identify the vertex, line of symmetry and intercepts of the parabola. (MP8)
standards.topic: Equivalence and Relational Thinking: Use concepts and properties of equivalence and relational thinking to represent and compare numerical expressions, proportional relationships, algebraic expressions and equations.

standards.description: Factor polynomials including common monomial factors from polynomials, quadratic polynomials and the difference of two squares. (MP7, MP8)
standards.topic: Equivalence and Relational Thinking: Use concepts and properties of equivalence and relational thinking to represent and compare numerical expressions, proportional relationships, algebraic expressions and equations.

standards.description: Solve quadratic equations by appropriate methods using factoring, completing the square, graphing or the quadratic formula. Find non-real complex roots when they exist. (MP2, MP7)
standards.topic: Equivalence and Relational Thinking: Use concepts and properties of equivalence and relational thinking to represent and compare numerical expressions, proportional relationships, algebraic expressions and equations.

standards.description: Compare the equation of a circle, the Pythagorean Theorem and the Distance Formula. Complete the square to find the center and radius of a circle given an equation. (MP2, MP8)
standards.topic: Equivalence and Relational Thinking: Use concepts and properties of equivalence and relational thinking to represent and compare numerical expressions, proportional relationships, algebraic expressions and equations.

standards.description: Solve situations involving relationships which are inversely proportional in various contexts, including rates. (MP4) ✜ $ ☼
standards.topic: Equivalence and Relational Thinking: Use concepts and properties of equivalence and relational thinking to represent and compare numerical expressions, proportional relationships, algebraic expressions and equations.

standards.description: Apply the properties of rational exponents and radicals to generate equivalent algebraic expressions. (MP2, MP7)
standards.topic: Equivalence and Relational Thinking: Use concepts and properties of equivalence and relational thinking to represent and compare numerical expressions, proportional relationships, algebraic expressions and equations.

standards.description: Represent and solve situations in various contexts, including financial literacy, using systems of linear equations, systems of linear inequalities and exponential and quadratic functions. (MP4) $ # ☼
standards.topic: Patterns and Relationships: Represent and connect mathematical patterns and relationships using verbal descriptions, generalizations, tables and graphs. Use representations to generate questions, make predictions and solve mathematical problems.

standards.description: Use the concept of a function as a connection between inputs and outputs to find function values and use function notation. (MP2)
standards.topic: Patterns and Relationships: Represent and connect mathematical patterns and relationships using verbal descriptions, generalizations, tables and graphs. Use representations to generate questions, make predictions and solve mathematical problems.

standards.description: Translate between graphs of quadratic, exponential and other functions (including absolute value, rational and polynomial), tables and symbolic representations. Sketch graphs and use graphing technology to graph functions. (MP5) $
standards.topic: Patterns and Relationships: Represent and connect mathematical patterns and relationships using verbal descriptions, generalizations, tables and graphs. Use representations to generate questions, make predictions and solve mathematical problems.

standards.description: Determine how vertical/ horizontal reflecting, translating and scaling affect the symbolic and graphical forms of a function. Use graphing technology to examine transformations. (MP3)
standards.topic: Patterns and Relationships: Represent and connect mathematical patterns and relationships using verbal descriptions, generalizations, tables and graphs. Use representations to generate questions, make predictions and solve mathematical problems.

standards.description: Express the terms in an arithmetic or geometric sequence recursively and by giving an explicit formula. (MP8) #
standards.topic: Patterns and Relationships: Represent and connect mathematical patterns and relationships using verbal descriptions, generalizations, tables and graphs. Use representations to generate questions, make predictions and solve mathematical problems.

standards.description: Express recursive patterns using recursive formulas. Calculate sequences defined by recursive formulas. (MP8) $
standards.topic: Patterns and Relationships: Represent and connect mathematical patterns and relationships using verbal descriptions, generalizations, tables and graphs. Use representations to generate questions, make predictions and solve mathematical problems.

standards.description: Find the domain and range of functions defined symbolically, graphically or in a context, including piecewise and step functions. Express solutions and recognize that some answers obtained may not be valid, including cases where the function inputs are discrete instead of continuous. (MP4) ✜ $ ☼
standards.topic: Patterns and Relationships: Represent and connect mathematical patterns and relationships using verbal descriptions, generalizations, tables and graphs. Use representations to generate questions, make predictions and solve mathematical problems.

standards.description: Describe the graph of a function using key features such as intercepts, maxima/minima, intervals of increase and decrease and end behavior. Draw conclusions from graphs of functions and other relations. (MP3) $ ☼
standards.topic: Patterns and Relationships: Represent and connect mathematical patterns and relationships using verbal descriptions, generalizations, tables and graphs. Use representations to generate questions, make predictions and solve mathematical problems.

standards.description: Define the compounding of interest n times per year according to a recursive formula. Compare the recursive definition of interest to the recursive definition of a geometric sequence t(n) = r(t(n – 1)). Compare the interest formula A = P(1 + r/n)^nt to the general form of an exponential function y = a(b)^x. Explain the purpose of each part of the interest formula. (MP4, MP5) $ # μ
standards.topic: Patterns and Relationships: Represent and connect mathematical patterns and relationships using verbal descriptions, generalizations, tables and graphs. Use representations to generate questions, make predictions and solve mathematical problems.

standards.description: Find the inverse of a given function and justify the results using tables, graphs or algebra. (MP4, MP6)
standards.topic: Patterns and Relationships: Represent and connect mathematical patterns and relationships using verbal descriptions, generalizations, tables and graphs. Use representations to generate questions, make predictions and solve mathematical problems.

standards.description: Notice and describe patterns in data-rich situations. (MP1, MP7) ✜ # ☼
standards.topic: Data Sciences: Identify, formulate and investigate statistical questions by collecting data considering cultural perspectives, analyzing and interpreting data and communicating the results.

standards.description: Organize objects, draw pictures or use tally marks to represent data and communicate observations. (MP3, MP6) # μ
standards.topic: Data Sciences: Identify, formulate and investigate statistical questions by collecting data considering cultural perspectives, analyzing and interpreting data and communicating the results.

standards.description: Compare objects with a measurable attribute in common, to see which object has “more of,” “less of” or the “same as” the attribute and explain the reasoning. (MP3, MP5) ✜ $ ☼
standards.topic: Measurement: Investigate measurement using a variety of tools, units, systems, processes and techniques in various cultures. Explain and reason with attributes, estimations and formulas to communicate measurement(s) and relationships effectively. Justify decisions and consider the reasonableness of the measurement.

standards.description: Describe several measurable attributes of objects such as length and weight. (MP4, MP6) ✜ ☼
standards.topic: Measurement: Investigate measurement using a variety of tools, units, systems, processes and techniques in various cultures. Explain and reason with attributes, estimations and formulas to communicate measurement(s) and relationships effectively. Justify decisions and consider the reasonableness of the measurement.

standards.description: Sort objects using characteristics such as shape, size, color and thickness. (MP1, MP3) ✜ ☼
standards.topic: Geometry: Analyze characteristics of geometric shapes to make mathematical arguments and justifications about geometric relationships. Use visualization and geometric modeling to compare, solve problems and communicate ideas.

standards.description: Identify and compare two- and three-dimensional shapes such as squares, circles, triangles, rectangles, trapezoids, hexagons, cubes, cones, cylinders and spheres using informal language to describe their similarities, differences, parts and other attributes. (MP2) ✜ ☼
standards.topic: Geometry: Analyze characteristics of geometric shapes to make mathematical arguments and justifications about geometric relationships. Use visualization and geometric modeling to compare, solve problems and communicate ideas.

standards.description: Compose, decompose and name simple shapes. Recognize shapes regardless of their overall size and orientation. (MP1, MP2) μ
standards.topic: Geometry: Analyze characteristics of geometric shapes to make mathematical arguments and justifications about geometric relationships. Use visualization and geometric modeling to compare, solve problems and communicate ideas.

standards.description: Describe objects in the environment using names of shapes. Describe the relative positions of these objects using terms such as above, below, beside, in front of, behind and next to. (MP1, MP6) ✜ ☼
standards.topic: Geometry: Analyze characteristics of geometric shapes to make mathematical arguments and justifications about geometric relationships. Use visualization and geometric modeling to compare, solve problems and communicate ideas.

standards.description: Recognize that a number can be used to represent how many objects are in a set or to represent the position of an object in a sequence. When counting objects, say the number names in the standard order, pairing each object with one and only one number name and each number with one and only one object. Understand that the last number said tells the number of objects counted. Understand that each successive number refers to a quantity that is one more. Name the position of an object in a sequence (ordinal count). (MP1, MP6) ✜ ☼
standards.topic: Number Relationships: Describe, Interpret and use quantities, relationships between quantities, representations of quantities, and number systems. Describe operations and the relationship between operations. Use strategies and procedures accurately, efficiently and flexibly. Assess the reasonableness of the results.

standards.description: Count collections of objects up to 31 by grouping in 10s using ten-frames, cups or other tools. (MP6, MP7) ✜ $ ☼
standards.topic: Number Relationships: Describe, Interpret and use quantities, relationships between quantities, representations of quantities, and number systems. Describe operations and the relationship between operations. Use strategies and procedures accurately, efficiently and flexibly. Assess the reasonableness of the results.

standards.description: Read, write, compare, order and represent whole numbers from 0 to at least 31 (with 0 representing the count of no objects) to answer the question “how many?”. Representations may include numerals, pictures, real objects, picture graphs, spoken words and manipulatives, such as connecting cubes. The numbers from 11 to 19 are composed of a 10 and one, two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight or nine ones. (MP4, MP8) ✜

standards.description: Count forward, with and without objects, to at least 31. Count backward from 20. (MP6) ☼

standards.description: Solve and represent a variety of addition and subtraction contextual situation types using objects, drawings, mental images or equations within 10. (MP4, MP5) $ μ
standards.topic: Number Relationships: Describe, Interpret and use quantities, relationships between quantities, representations of quantities, and number systems. Describe operations and the relationship between operations. Use strategies and procedures accurately, efficiently and flexibly. Assess the reasonableness of the results.

standards.description: Compose and decompose numbers less than or equal to 10 into pairs in more than one way with objects and pictures. Record each decomposition with a drawing or equation. (MP7)
standards.topic: Number Relationships: Describe, Interpret and use quantities, relationships between quantities, representations of quantities, and number systems. standards.description: Fluently add and subtract within 5. (MP2)
standards.topic: Number Relationships: Describe, Interpret and use quantities, relationships between quantities, representations of quantities, and number systems. standards.description: Identify whether the number of objects in one group is greater than, less than or equal to the number of objects in another group (by using matching, counting strategies and a number line). (MP2, MP5) ☼

standards.description: Recognize that the equal sign (=) is a comparison symbol of two math expressions of equal value number. (MP6)
standards.topic: Equivalence and Relational Thinking: Use concepts and properties of equivalence and relational thinking to represent and compare numerical expressions, proportional relationships, algebraic expressions and equations.

standards.description: Recognize, create, complete and extend simple patterns using shape, color, size, number, sounds and movements. Patterns may be repeating, growing or shrinking. (MP1, MP7) ✜ # ☼
standards.topic: Patterns and Relationships: Represent and connect mathematical patterns and relationships using verbal descriptions, generalizations, tables and graphs. Use representations to generate questions, make predictions and solve mathematical problems.

topic_clusters.name: Addition & Subtraction within 20
topic_clusters.level4_desc: Adds and subtracts within 20 using mental strategies (doubles, near doubles, make 10, count on). Solves all problem types (result unknown, change unknown, start unknown). Writes and explains equations using the equal sign correctly.
topic_clusters.level3_desc: Adds and subtracts within 20 using objects, drawings, or strategies (counting on, making 10). Solves one-step word problems for result-unknown and change-unknown types. Understands the meaning of the equal sign.
topic_clusters.level2_desc: Adds and subtracts within 10 reliably; struggles with teen numbers. Uses counting-all strategy (not yet counting on). Solves result-unknown word problems with manipulatives.
topic_clusters.level1_desc: Adds within 5 using fingers or objects with some accuracy. Cannot yet subtract reliably. Needs direct modeling to connect word problems to operations.
topic_clusters.tier3_vocab: difference, fact family, doubles, make ten, equation, equal sign

topic_clusters.name: Data & Graphs
topic_clusters.level4_desc: Collects data, creates tally charts and picture/bar-type graphs independently. Answers comparison questions (how many more, how many fewer) using the data. Generates original questions from a data display.
topic_clusters.level3_desc: Collects, sorts, and organizes data into up to three categories. Reads tally charts and picture/bar-type graphs to answer simple questions. Draws conclusions from data displays.
topic_clusters.level2_desc: Sorts data into given categories and counts with support. Reads individual values from a picture graph but struggles with comparison questions.
topic_clusters.level1_desc: Participates in data sorting with direct guidance. Cannot yet independently read or interpret simple graphs.
topic_clusters.tier2_vocab: chart, table, picture graph, bar graph
topic_clusters.tier3_vocab: tally, more, fewer, how many

topic_clusters.name: Geometry & Spatial Reasoning
topic_clusters.level4_desc: Distinguishes defining vs. non-defining attributes of shapes. Composes and decomposes 2-D and 3-D shapes to create new shapes. Partitions circles and rectangles into halves and fourths, names the parts, and understands the whole is two or four of the parts.
topic_clusters.level3_desc: Identifies and describes 2-D shapes by their attributes (number of sides, corners). Builds and draws shapes with specified attributes. Partitions circles and rectangles into two or four equal shares and describes them as halves/fourths.
topic_clusters.level2_desc: Names common 2-D shapes. Identifies the number of sides or corners with support. Partitions shapes into halves when shown a model but struggles with fourths.
topic_clusters.level1_desc: Recognizes basic shapes (circle, square) but cannot describe their attributes. Cannot yet partition shapes into equal parts without direct modeling.
topic_clusters.tier3_vocab: sides, vertices, corners, 2-D, 3-D, partition, halves, fourths, quarters

topic_clusters.name: Measurement, Time & Data
topic_clusters.level4_desc: Measures lengths using same-size units without gaps or overlaps and expresses the measurement as a number. Orders three or more objects by length. Tells time to the hour and half hour on analog and digital clocks. Organizes, represents, and interprets data with up to three categories.
topic_clusters.level3_desc: Measures length by laying multiple copies of a shorter object end to end. Compares the lengths of two objects using a third object. Tells time to the hour and half-hour. Collects and organizes data into categories and reads simple graphs.
topic_clusters.level2_desc: Compares two objects directly (longer/shorter). Uses non-standard units with some gaps/overlaps. Tells time to the hour on a digital clock. Participates in data collection but needs help reading graphs.
topic_clusters.level1_desc: Uses vague language for length comparison. Cannot yet iterate measurement units. Does not reliably read an analog clock. Needs significant support with data activities.
topic_clusters.tier3_vocab: length, longer, shorter, hour, half hour, tally

topic_clusters.name: Place Value (Tens & Ones)
topic_clusters.level4_desc: Counts, reads, and writes numbers to 120. Understands that the two digits of a two-digit number represent amounts of tens and ones. Uses place value understanding to compare and order numbers using >, <, =. Adds and subtracts multiples of 10 mentally.
topic_clusters.level3_desc: Represents numbers to 120 using objects, drawings, and expanded/standard form. Identifies the tens and ones in a two-digit number. Compares two two-digit numbers using place value.
topic_clusters.level2_desc: Counts to 100 with some errors beyond 50. Identifies tens and ones with concrete models (base-ten blocks) but struggles with symbolic representation. Compares numbers with support.
topic_clusters.level1_desc: Counts to 40–50 with errors. Does not yet reliably distinguish tens from ones. Needs base-ten blocks and significant scaffolding to represent two-digit numbers.
topic_clusters.tier3_vocab: tens, ones, place value, digit, expanded form

topic_clusters.name: Addition & Subtraction within 1,000
topic_clusters.level4_desc: Adds and subtracts within 1,000 using strategies based on place value, properties of operations, and the relationship between addition and subtraction. Solves multi-step word problems. Fluently adds and subtracts within 100 mentally.
topic_clusters.level3_desc: Fluently adds and subtracts within 20 using mental strategies. Adds and subtracts within 100 using place-value strategies. Adds up to four two-digit numbers. Solves one- and two-step word problems using addition and subtraction.
topic_clusters.level2_desc: Adds and subtracts within 100 using concrete models or drawings. Solves one-step word problems with some support. Still developing fluency within 20.
topic_clusters.level1_desc: Adds and subtracts within 20 using counting strategies (not yet fluent). Needs manipulatives for problems beyond 20. Struggles to interpret word problems independently.
topic_clusters.tier3_vocab: borrow, carry, algorithm, mental math, equation, sum, difference

topic_clusters.name: Data Analysis & Graphs
topic_clusters.level4_desc: Designs simple surveys, collects data, and creates appropriate displays (line plots, bar graphs). Interprets data to answer "how many more" and "how many less" questions. Draws conclusions and makes predictions.
topic_clusters.level3_desc: Creates and reads line plots, picture graphs, and bar graphs. Answers questions requiring addition and subtraction of data values. Organizes data into categories and represents it accurately.
topic_clusters.level2_desc: Reads values from graphs with support. Creates simple picture graphs with guidance. Answers "how many" but struggles with comparison questions.
topic_clusters.level1_desc: Identifies what a graph shows (topic) but cannot reliably extract specific values. Needs direct support to create any data display.
topic_clusters.tier2_vocab: data, bar graph, picture graph, scale, category
topic_clusters.tier3_vocab: line plot, survey, most, least

topic_clusters.name: Geometry & Fractions Introduction
topic_clusters.level4_desc: Identifies and draws shapes with specified attributes (number of faces, edges, angles). Partitions rectangles into rows and columns of same-size squares and counts to find the total. Describes shares using the language of halves, thirds, and fourths, recognizing that equal shares of identical wholes need not have the same shape.
topic_clusters.level3_desc: Recognizes and classifies 2-D and 3-D shapes by their attributes. Partitions circles and rectangles into two, three, or four equal shares. Uses fraction language (halves, thirds, fourths) to describe the shares.
topic_clusters.level2_desc: Identifies basic 2-D and 3-D shapes. Partitions shapes into halves but struggles with thirds. Uses fraction language with support.
topic_clusters.level1_desc: Names common shapes but cannot describe their attributes. Cannot yet partition shapes into equal parts independently.
topic_clusters.tier3_vocab: angle, partition, halves, thirds, fourths, equal shares

topic_clusters.name: Measurement & Data
topic_clusters.level4_desc: Measures objects using standard units (inches, feet, centimeters, meters) and selects appropriate tools. Estimates lengths before measuring. Represents data on line plots, picture graphs, and bar graphs, then solves comparison problems using the data.
topic_clusters.level3_desc: Measures the length of objects using rulers and standard units. Compares lengths of two objects and expresses the difference. Generates and reads data from line plots and bar graphs.
topic_clusters.level2_desc: Measures length with a ruler with some alignment errors. Reads values from simple graphs but struggles with comparison questions. Needs prompts to estimate before measuring.
topic_clusters.level1_desc: Attempts to measure with a ruler but makes significant alignment errors. Can read individual values from a picture graph with support but cannot compare or draw conclusions.
topic_clusters.tier3_vocab: inch, foot, centimeter, meter, length, line plot

topic_clusters.name: Place Value to 1,000
topic_clusters.level4_desc: Reads, writes, and represents numbers to 1,200 using expanded, standard, and word forms. Flexibly skip counts by 2s, 5s, 10s, and 100s from any starting number. Compares and orders three-digit numbers using place value reasoning and symbols (>, <, =).
topic_clusters.level3_desc: Reads and writes numbers to 1,000 in multiple forms. Understands that the three digits of a three-digit number represent amounts of hundreds, tens, and ones. Compares two three-digit numbers using >, <, =.
topic_clusters.level2_desc: Reads and writes numbers to 400–500. Identifies hundreds, tens, and ones with base-ten blocks. Compares numbers when hundreds digit differs but struggles when hundreds are the same.
topic_clusters.level1_desc: Reads and writes numbers to 100. Identifies tens and ones but not hundreds. Needs concrete models to compare or represent numbers beyond 100.
topic_clusters.tier3_vocab: hundreds, tens, ones, expanded form, standard form, word form, skip count

topic_clusters.name: Area & Perimeter
topic_clusters.level4_desc: Finds area by multiplying side lengths and by tiling, connecting both methods. Solves real-world problems involving area and perimeter. Recognizes that rectangles with the same area can have different perimeters, and vice versa. Decomposes rectilinear figures to find area.
topic_clusters.level3_desc: Measures area by counting unit squares. Finds the area of a rectangle by multiplying side lengths. Finds the perimeter of a polygon by adding side lengths. Solves problems involving perimeter when one side is missing.
topic_clusters.level2_desc: Counts unit squares to find area with some errors (miscounting corners). Finds perimeter by adding all labeled sides. Confuses area and perimeter when both are asked in the same problem.
topic_clusters.level1_desc: Understands area as "covering" and perimeter as "around" but cannot independently calculate either. Needs grid overlays and labeled diagrams to attempt measurement.
topic_clusters.tier3_vocab: area, perimeter, tile, formula, length, width, side

topic_clusters.name: Data, Graphs & Measurement
topic_clusters.level4_desc: Creates scaled bar graphs and pictographs where each symbol represents multiple items. Generates measurement data by measuring to the nearest 1/4 inch and displays results on line plots. Solves multi-step "how many more" and "how many fewer" problems using graph data.
topic_clusters.level3_desc: Draws and reads scaled picture graphs and bar graphs. Solves one- and two-step comparison problems using bar graph data. Measures objects to the nearest half inch and records data on a line plot.
topic_clusters.level2_desc: Reads scaled graphs with guidance on the scale. Measures to the nearest inch. Creates bar graphs with 1:1 scale. Answers simple questions from data displays.
topic_clusters.level1_desc: Reads unscaled bar graphs for individual values. Cannot yet create graphs independently. Measures with a ruler but has difficulty with precision beyond whole inches.
topic_clusters.tier2_vocab: scaled bar graph, scaled picture graph, measure
topic_clusters.tier3_vocab: line plot, fraction, nearest half, quarter inch

topic_clusters.name: Fractions
topic_clusters.level4_desc: Represents fractions on number lines and with area models. Generates simple equivalent fractions (e.g., 1/2 = 2/4) and explains why they are equivalent. Compares fractions with the same numerator or same denominator using reasoning about size. Expresses whole numbers as fractions and fractions equal to whole numbers.
topic_clusters.level3_desc: Understands a fraction 1/b as one part when a whole is partitioned into b equal parts. Represents fractions with denominators 2, 3, 4, 6, and 8 using models and number lines. Compares two fractions with the same denominator or same numerator.
topic_clusters.level2_desc: Identifies fractions of a whole using shaded region models (halves, fourths). Places fractions on a number line with support. Compares fractions with same denominator when using visual models.
topic_clusters.level1_desc: Recognizes that a fraction represents part of a whole but cannot yet identify specific fractions reliably. Confuses numerator and denominator. Needs physical models to visualize any fraction.
topic_clusters.tier3_vocab: fraction, numerator, denominator, equivalent, whole, number line, equal parts

topic_clusters.name: Geometry & Shape Attributes
topic_clusters.level4_desc: Classifies shapes by multiple attributes simultaneously and recognizes that shared attributes can define larger categories (e.g., quadrilaterals include rectangles, rhombuses, squares). Draws shapes that satisfy given conditions. Partitions shapes into parts with equal areas and expresses area of each part as a unit fraction.
topic_clusters.level3_desc: Recognizes that shapes in different categories may share attributes. Classifies quadrilaterals by properties (sides, angles). Partitions shapes into parts with equal areas.
topic_clusters.level2_desc: Sorts shapes by one attribute at a time. Identifies quadrilaterals but struggles to subcategorize them. Partitions shapes into equal parts with visual support.
topic_clusters.level1_desc: Names common shapes but cannot describe defining attributes. Sorting is inconsistent. Needs models to understand equal partitioning.
topic_clusters.tier3_vocab: quadrilateral, rhombus, polygon, partition, equal areas, fraction of shape

topic_clusters.name: Multiplication & Division
topic_clusters.level4_desc: Fluently multiplies and divides within 100. Solves two-step word problems using all four operations. Applies properties of multiplication (commutative, associative, distributive) to solve problems and justify strategies. Identifies arithmetic patterns and explains them using properties of operations.
topic_clusters.level3_desc: Uses strategies to multiply and divide within 100 (arrays, equal groups, skip counting, known facts). Solves one-step word problems involving multiplication and division. Understands the relationship between multiplication and division.
topic_clusters.level2_desc: Multiplies within 50 using arrays or repeated addition. Solves multiplication word problems with concrete support. Recalls some basic facts (×2, ×5, ×10) but not yet fluent.
topic_clusters.level1_desc: Understands multiplication as equal groups when modeled. Counts by 2s and 5s. Cannot yet solve multiplication problems without concrete materials or direct guidance.
topic_clusters.tier3_vocab: multiply, divide, factor, product, quotient, array, commutative, distributive, fluency

topic_clusters.name: Decimals & Place Value
topic_clusters.level4_desc: Converts fluently between fractions with denominators of 10 or 100 and their decimal equivalents. Compares and orders decimals to hundredths using number lines, place value, and benchmark fractions. Adds fractions with denominators of 10 and 100 using decimal understanding.
topic_clusters.level3_desc: Expresses fractions with denominator 10 as equivalent fractions with denominator 100 and writes as decimals. Uses decimal notation for fractions with denominators 10 or 100. Compares two decimals to hundredths using visual models or place value.
topic_clusters.level2_desc: Reads and writes decimals to tenths. Identifies the decimal equivalent of 1/10 and 1/100 with models. Compares decimals to tenths but struggles with hundredths.
topic_clusters.level1_desc: Recognizes that a decimal point separates wholes from parts but cannot reliably read or write decimals. Needs fraction models to see the connection between fractions and decimals.
topic_clusters.tier3_vocab: decimal, tenths, hundredths, decimal point, fraction, equivalent, notation

topic_clusters.name: Fractions & Equivalence
topic_clusters.level4_desc: Generates equivalent fractions using multiplication and division and explains why. Compares fractions with unlike denominators using benchmark fractions (0, 1/2, 1) and common denominators. Adds and subtracts fractions and mixed numbers with like denominators, including in word problems. Decomposes a fraction into a sum of fractions in more than one way.
topic_clusters.level3_desc: Recognizes and generates equivalent fractions using visual models and multiplication. Compares two fractions with different numerators and different denominators using visual models or common denominators. Adds and subtracts fractions with the same denominator.
topic_clusters.level2_desc: Identifies equivalent fractions when shown visual models. Compares fractions with the same denominator. Adds fractions with like denominators using models but struggles with symbolic computation.
topic_clusters.level1_desc: Recognizes simple equivalent fractions (1/2 = 2/4) with visual support. Cannot yet compare fractions with unlike denominators. Needs fraction strips or circles to perform any operations.
topic_clusters.tier3_vocab: equivalent fraction, benchmark fraction, mixed number, improper fraction, common denominator, numerator, denominator

topic_clusters.name: Geometry, Angles & Symmetry
topic_clusters.level4_desc: Measures and draws angles using a protractor with accuracy. Solves addition and subtraction problems to find unknown angles on a diagram. Identifies and draws lines of symmetry for a variety of figures. Classifies 2-D figures based on parallel or perpendicular sides, and angle types.
topic_clusters.level3_desc: Recognizes angles as geometric shapes and measures them in whole-number degrees. Understands angle measurement as additive. Identifies and draws lines of symmetry. Classifies triangles by angle type and quadrilaterals by presence of parallel/perpendicular sides.
topic_clusters.level2_desc: Uses a protractor with some errors. Identifies right, acute, and obtuse angles. Recognizes lines of symmetry in simple shapes. Classifies some figures by properties with support.
topic_clusters.level1_desc: Recognizes right angles but confuses acute and obtuse. Cannot yet use a protractor independently. Identifies symmetry in simple cases (butterfly, heart) but not in geometric figures.
topic_clusters.tier3_vocab: obtuse, straight, protractor, perpendicular, parallel, line of symmetry, ray, point, line, line segment

topic_clusters.name: Measurement & Unit Conversion
topic_clusters.level4_desc: Converts between units within the same measurement system using multi-step reasoning (e.g., 3 ft 7 in = 43 in). Solves word problems involving distance, time, liquid volume, mass, and money including simple fractions or decimals. Makes a line plot of measurements to fractions of a unit (1/2, 1/4, 1/8).
topic_clusters.level3_desc: Knows relative sizes of measurement units within one system (e.g., 1 ft = 12 in, 1 kg = 1,000 g). Uses the four operations to solve measurement word problems involving simple fractions or decimals. Converts larger units to smaller units within the same system.
topic_clusters.level2_desc: Identifies common unit equivalences with a reference chart. Solves one-step measurement problems. Converts units when given the conversion factor but struggles to recall relationships independently.
topic_clusters.level1_desc: Recognizes measurement units but does not reliably know equivalences. Needs a conversion chart and step-by-step guidance to solve measurement problems.
topic_clusters.tier3_vocab: customary, metric, inch, foot, yard, mile, gram, kilogram, liter, milliliter, elapsed time, interval

topic_clusters.name: Multi-Digit Multiplication & Division
topic_clusters.level4_desc: Multiplies up to 4-digit × 1-digit and 2-digit × 2-digit using the standard algorithm and area models. Divides with up to 4-digit dividends and 1-digit divisors, interpreting remainders in context. Solves multi-step word problems and assesses reasonableness using estimation.
topic_clusters.level3_desc: Multiplies a 4-digit number by a 1-digit number using strategies including the standard algorithm. Finds whole-number quotients and remainders with up to 4-digit dividends. Solves word problems involving multiplication and division, including interpreting remainders.
topic_clusters.level2_desc: Multiplies 2-digit × 1-digit with accuracy; 3- and 4-digit multiplication contains errors. Divides with 2-digit dividends and 1-digit divisors. Solves one-step word problems with support.
topic_clusters.level1_desc: Multiplies 1-digit × 1-digit fluently but struggles with multi-digit multiplication. Uses repeated subtraction for division with small numbers. Needs direct instruction on algorithms.
topic_clusters.tier3_vocab: product, quotient, remainder, algorithm, partial products, factor, multiple

topic_clusters.name: Algebraic Thinking & Patterns
topic_clusters.level4_desc: Writes, interprets, and evaluates numerical expressions with parentheses, brackets, and braces following order of operations. Generates two numerical patterns using two given rules, forms ordered pairs, and graphs them on a coordinate plane. Explains the relationship between corresponding terms.
topic_clusters.level3_desc: Writes and interprets simple expressions without evaluating them (e.g., "add 8 and 7, then multiply by 2" as 2 × (8 + 7)). Evaluates expressions with parentheses. Generates and analyzes numerical patterns.
topic_clusters.level2_desc: Evaluates expressions with one set of parentheses. Identifies and extends simple numerical patterns. Writes expressions for basic situations with support.
topic_clusters.level1_desc: Follows order of operations for expressions without grouping symbols. Identifies repeating patterns. Needs direct instruction to write or evaluate expressions with parentheses.
topic_clusters.tier3_vocab: expression, variable, parentheses, brackets, braces, order of operations

topic_clusters.name: Coordinate Plane
topic_clusters.level4_desc: Graphs and interprets ordered pairs in the first quadrant to solve real-world and mathematical problems. Identifies patterns in coordinate data and explains relationships between corresponding terms of two numerical patterns. Generates ordered pairs from two rules and graphs them.
topic_clusters.level3_desc: Identifies the x-axis, y-axis, and origin. Graphs ordered pairs in the first quadrant. Interprets coordinate values in the context of a problem. Represents real-world situations by graphing in the first quadrant.
topic_clusters.level2_desc: Plots points in the first quadrant when given ordered pairs. Reads coordinates from a graph with support. Sometimes reverses x and y coordinates.
topic_clusters.level1_desc: Identifies axes and origin when labeled. Cannot yet independently plot or read ordered pairs from a coordinate plane.
topic_clusters.tier3_vocab: coordinate plane, x-axis, y-axis, origin, ordered pair, quadrant, plot

topic_clusters.name: Decimals — Operations & Place Value
topic_clusters.level4_desc: Multiplies and divides decimals to hundredths fluently. Uses patterns in powers of 10 to explain the placement of the decimal point. Reads, writes, and compares decimals to thousandths in multiple forms. Solves real-world decimal problems and estimates to check reasonableness.
topic_clusters.level3_desc: Reads and writes decimals to thousandths using expanded notation. Compares and orders decimals to thousandths using place value. Performs all four operations with decimals to hundredths using concrete models or strategies.
topic_clusters.level2_desc: Reads and compares decimals to hundredths. Adds and subtracts decimals by lining up place values. Multiplies a decimal by a whole number with support. Division of decimals is emerging.
topic_clusters.level1_desc: Reads and writes decimals to tenths. Adds decimals with tenths only. Struggles with hundredths and thousandths. Needs base-ten models or grids to perform any decimal operations.
topic_clusters.tier3_vocab: thousandths, hundredths, tenths, place value, expanded notation, powers of ten, multiply, divide, decimal

topic_clusters.name: Fractions — All Operations
topic_clusters.level4_desc: Adds, subtracts, multiplies, and divides fractions and mixed numbers fluently. Solves multi-step word problems involving fractions. Uses benchmark fractions and estimation to check reasonableness. Explains why multiplication by a fraction less than 1 results in a product less than the other factor.
topic_clusters.level3_desc: Adds and subtracts fractions with unlike denominators by finding common denominators. Multiplies fractions and mixed numbers. Divides unit fractions by whole numbers and whole numbers by unit fractions. Solves word problems involving fraction operations.
topic_clusters.level2_desc: Adds and subtracts fractions with like denominators fluently; struggles with unlike denominators. Multiplies a fraction by a whole number. Divides with fractions using visual models with support.
topic_clusters.level1_desc: Adds fractions with like denominators. Identifies common denominators with a reference tool. Cannot yet multiply or divide fractions independently.
topic_clusters.tier3_vocab: common denominator, equivalent fraction, mixed number, improper fraction, reciprocal, product, quotient, benchmark

topic_clusters.name: Volume
topic_clusters.level4_desc: Finds the volume of rectangular prisms by packing with unit cubes and by applying V = l×w×h and V = B×h. Finds volumes of composite solid figures by decomposing into non-overlapping rectangular prisms. Connects volume to multiplication and addition in multi-step problems.
topic_clusters.level3_desc: Recognizes volume as an attribute of solid figures measured in cubic units. Measures volume by counting unit cubes. Finds the volume of a right rectangular prism using the formulas V = l×w×h and V = B×h.
topic_clusters.level2_desc: Counts unit cubes to find volume of simple rectangular prisms. Uses V = l×w×h with whole-number dimensions when prompted. Struggles with composite figures.
topic_clusters.level1_desc: Understands volume means "how much space inside" but cannot reliably count unit cubes or apply formulas. Confuses area and volume.
topic_clusters.tier3_vocab: volume, rectangular prism, length, width, height, formula, V = l × w × h, V = B × h

topic_clusters.name: Area & Geometry
topic_clusters.level4_desc: Finds the area of triangles, quadrilaterals, and polygons by decomposing into triangles and rectangles. Finds the volume of right rectangular prisms with fractional edge lengths. Uses nets to find surface area. Solves real-world area and volume problems.
topic_clusters.level3_desc: Applies area formulas for triangles, parallelograms, and trapezoids. Finds the volume of a right rectangular prism. Represents 3-D figures using nets and finds surface area.
topic_clusters.level2_desc: Finds the area of rectangles and triangles using formulas. Finds the volume of rectangular prisms with whole-number dimensions. Uses nets with guidance to find surface area.
topic_clusters.level1_desc: Finds the area of rectangles. Counts unit cubes for volume. Cannot independently apply area formulas for triangles or parallelograms.
topic_clusters.tier3_vocab: area, triangle, parallelogram, trapezoid, polygon, net, surface area, volume, base, height

topic_clusters.name: Coordinate Plane
topic_clusters.level4_desc: Graphs points in all four quadrants and solves real-world problems involving coordinate geometry. Finds distances between points on a coordinate plane using absolute value. Draws polygons given coordinates and determines side lengths.
topic_clusters.level3_desc: Identifies and graphs ordered pairs in all four quadrants. Understands signs of coordinates indicate direction. Finds horizontal and vertical distances between points with the same x- or y-coordinate.
topic_clusters.level2_desc: Graphs points in the first quadrant fluently. Graphs in other quadrants with some sign errors. Finds distances with support.
topic_clusters.level1_desc: Graphs points in the first quadrant. Struggles with negative coordinates. Cannot yet determine distances between points.
topic_clusters.tier3_vocab: y-axis, origin, ordered pair, reflection, distance, absolute value

topic_clusters.name: Expressions & Equations
topic_clusters.level4_desc: Writes and evaluates expressions involving exponents and the order of operations. Writes and solves equations and inequalities in real-world contexts. Identifies and generates equivalent expressions using properties of operations (commutative, associative, distributive).
topic_clusters.level3_desc: Writes, reads, and evaluates algebraic expressions. Solves one-step equations (x + p = q, px = q) using inverse operations. Writes one-variable inequalities and represents solutions on number lines.
topic_clusters.level2_desc: Evaluates expressions by substituting given values. Solves one-step equations using guess-and-check or concrete models. Identifies variables in an expression.
topic_clusters.level1_desc: Understands that a letter represents an unknown number. Evaluates simple expressions with one operation. Cannot yet write or solve equations independently.
topic_clusters.tier3_vocab: variable, expression, equation, inequality, coefficient, constant, substitute, solve, inverse operation

topic_clusters.name: Fractions, Decimals & Percents
topic_clusters.level4_desc: Divides fractions by fractions fluently and interprets quotients in context. Converts between fractions, decimals, and percents flexibly to solve problems. Divides multi-digit numbers and performs all operations on multi-digit decimals fluently.
topic_clusters.level3_desc: Interprets and computes quotients of fractions. Fluently divides multi-digit numbers using the standard algorithm. Fluently adds, subtracts, multiplies, and divides multi-digit decimals.
topic_clusters.level2_desc: Divides a fraction by a whole number using models. Performs operations on multi-digit decimals with some errors. Converts between fractions and decimals for common equivalents (1/2, 1/4).
topic_clusters.level1_desc: Multiplies fractions but struggles with division of fractions. Adds/subtracts decimals with alignment support. Limited ability to convert between forms.
topic_clusters.tier3_vocab: reciprocal, quotient, dividend, divisor, algorithm, decimal, percent, fluency

topic_clusters.name: Integers & Rational Numbers
topic_clusters.level4_desc: Orders and compares rational numbers in all forms (fractions, decimals, negatives) on a number line. Solves real-world problems requiring understanding of absolute value and opposites. Explains the relationship between a number and its opposite and between a number and its absolute value.
topic_clusters.level3_desc: Locates positive and negative integers and rational numbers on a number line. Understands ordering of rational numbers, including negative numbers. Finds the absolute value of a rational number and interprets it as distance from 0.
topic_clusters.level2_desc: Locates positive integers and some negative integers on a number line. Compares integers using the number line. Finds absolute value of integers but struggles with rational numbers.
topic_clusters.level1_desc: Recognizes positive and negative numbers. Can place whole numbers on a number line but struggles with negatives and fractions. Needs visual support for absolute value concept.
topic_clusters.tier3_vocab: negative, absolute value, opposite, rational number, number line, inequality

topic_clusters.name: Percents
topic_clusters.level4_desc: Solves multi-step percent problems including finding the whole given a part and the percent. Converts fluently among fractions, decimals, and percents to choose the most efficient form for a given problem.
topic_clusters.level3_desc: Finds a percent of a quantity as a rate per 100. Solves problems involving finding the whole, given a part and the percent. Represents and converts benchmark percents (10%, 25%, 50%, 75%) as fractions and decimals.
topic_clusters.level2_desc: Finds simple percents of numbers (10%, 50%) using models. Converts between common percents and fractions. Struggles with problems requiring percent to find the whole.
topic_clusters.level1_desc: Understands percent means "out of 100" but cannot calculate percentages independently. Needs hundred grids to visualize percent.
topic_clusters.tier3_vocab: whole, part, equivalent, fraction, decimal, proportion

topic_clusters.name: Ratios & Rates
topic_clusters.level4_desc: Uses ratio and rate reasoning to solve complex real-world problems using tables, tape diagrams, double number lines, and equations. Makes conversions between measurement systems. Compares ratios in different ways and determines which is the better deal.
topic_clusters.level3_desc: Understands the concept of a ratio and uses ratio language. Finds unit rates. Uses ratio tables, double number lines, and tape diagrams to solve ratio and rate problems.
topic_clusters.level2_desc: Identifies ratios in simple contexts. Finds equivalent ratios using multiplication tables. Calculates unit rate with support. Represents ratios as fractions.
topic_clusters.level1_desc: Recognizes that a ratio compares two quantities. Can create equivalent ratios for simple cases (e.g., 1:2, 2:4) but cannot yet solve ratio problems independently.
topic_clusters.tier3_vocab: ratio, rate, equivalent ratio, proportion, per, tape diagram, double number line

topic_clusters.name: Statistics & Data
topic_clusters.level4_desc: Summarizes and interprets data distributions using measures of center (mean, median) and variability (IQR, MAD). Compares distributions using appropriate displays and statistics. Recognizes the effect of outliers on mean vs. median.
topic_clusters.level3_desc: Displays data in dot plots, histograms, and box plots. Calculates mean, median, and range. Describes the center, spread, and shape of data distributions. Summarizes data sets in relation to context.
topic_clusters.level2_desc: Creates dot plots and calculates mean and median. Identifies the range. Reads box plots and histograms with support. Describes distributions as symmetric or skewed.
topic_clusters.level1_desc: Calculates the mean of a small data set. Reads values from dot plots. Cannot yet create histograms or box plots independently.
topic_clusters.tier3_vocab: interquartile range, IQR, dot plot, histogram, box plot, distribution, center, spread, variability, MAD

topic_clusters.name: Area, Surface Area & Volume
topic_clusters.level4_desc: Solves real-world problems involving area, surface area, and volume of 3-D figures including composite shapes. Describes cross-sections of 3-D figures. Computes lateral and total surface area of prisms and pyramids. Finds area of composite figures with semicircles.
topic_clusters.level3_desc: Solves problems involving area of composite figures (rectangles, triangles, circles). Finds the volume and surface area of rectangular and triangular prisms and pyramids. Uses nets to compute surface area.
topic_clusters.level2_desc: Finds the area of basic figures and volume of rectangular prisms. Calculates surface area of rectangular prisms using nets with support. Struggles with pyramids and composite figures.
topic_clusters.level1_desc: Finds the area of rectangles and triangles. Finds volume of rectangular prisms by counting cubes. Cannot yet compute surface area independently.
topic_clusters.tier3_vocab: volume, prism, pyramid, composite figure, net, cross-section, base, height

topic_clusters.name: Circles & Circumference
topic_clusters.level4_desc: Solves real-world and mathematical problems involving area and circumference of circles and composite figures including semicircles and quarter circles. Explains the relationship between circumference and diameter (π) and derives the area formula using reasoning.
topic_clusters.level3_desc: Knows the formulas for circumference (C = πd or C = 2πr) and area (A = πr²) and uses them to solve problems. Describes π as the ratio of circumference to diameter. Uses 3.14 or 22/7 as approximations.
topic_clusters.level2_desc: Finds circumference and area of circles when given the radius and reminded of the formulas. Confuses radius and diameter in some problems.
topic_clusters.level1_desc: Identifies radius, diameter, and circumference on a circle diagram. Cannot yet apply formulas independently to find circumference or area.
topic_clusters.tier3_vocab: radius, pi, π, area, C = πd, C = 2πr, A = πr²

topic_clusters.name: Equations & Inequalities
topic_clusters.level4_desc: Solves multi-step real-world and mathematical problems with positive and negative rational numbers. Writes and solves two-step equations and inequalities in the form px + q = r and px + q > r. Uses properties of operations to generate equivalent expressions and justify solution steps.
topic_clusters.level3_desc: Writes and solves two-step equations and inequalities. Represents solutions for inequalities on number lines. Applies properties of operations to add, subtract, factor, and expand linear expressions.
topic_clusters.level2_desc: Solves two-step equations with whole numbers. Writes equations from word problems with support. Graphs inequality solutions on a number line with direction prompts.
topic_clusters.level1_desc: Solves one-step equations. Recognizes that an inequality allows many solutions. Needs guided steps to set up or solve two-step problems.
topic_clusters.tier3_vocab: coefficient, constant, like terms, distributive property, two-step, solution, constraint

topic_clusters.name: Operations with Rational Numbers
topic_clusters.level4_desc: Adds, subtracts, multiplies, and divides rational numbers fluently (including complex fractions). Solves real-world problems requiring all four operations with positive and negative rational numbers. Interprets sums, products, quotients, and differences of rational numbers in context.
topic_clusters.level3_desc: Performs all four operations with integers and rational numbers (fractions, decimals). Understands p + q as the number located |q| units from p in the positive or negative direction. Converts between fraction and decimal forms of rational numbers.
topic_clusters.level2_desc: Adds and subtracts integers using a number line or rules. Multiplies integers. Struggles with division of negative fractions. Converts simple fractions to decimals.
topic_clusters.level1_desc: Adds positive and negative integers with a number line. Multiplies positive integers fluently but is uncertain with negatives. Cannot yet operate with negative fractions/decimals.
topic_clusters.tier3_vocab: additive inverse, absolute value, terminating decimal, repeating decimal, opposite, distance

topic_clusters.name: Percent Problems
topic_clusters.level4_desc: Solves multi-step percent problems including successive percent changes, simple interest, and tax/tip/commission scenarios. Uses proportional reasoning to find the original amount given the result of a percent change.
topic_clusters.level3_desc: Solves problems involving percent increase, percent decrease, markups, markdowns, tax, tip, and simple interest. Uses proportional relationships (part/whole = percent/100) to find any unknown.
topic_clusters.level2_desc: Calculates simple percent of a number. Solves percent increase or decrease with support. Can set up a proportion but may make errors solving.
topic_clusters.level1_desc: Finds 10% and 50% of a number. Understands percent means "per hundred" but cannot solve percent change problems without step-by-step guidance.
topic_clusters.tier2_vocab: percent increase, percent decrease, percent change
topic_clusters.tier3_vocab: markup, markdown, tax, tip, discount, commission, simple interest, principal

topic_clusters.name: Probability
topic_clusters.level4_desc: Designs and uses simulations to generate frequencies for compound events. Compares experimental and theoretical probabilities, explaining sources of discrepancy. Finds probabilities of compound events using organized lists, tables, tree diagrams, and the counting principle.
topic_clusters.level3_desc: Finds theoretical and experimental probabilities of simple events. Represents sample spaces for compound events using tree diagrams and organized lists. Understands that probability is between 0 and 1 and identifies complementary events.
topic_clusters.level2_desc: Finds theoretical probability of simple events. Creates sample spaces for simple compound events with guidance. Understands that probability of 0 is impossible and 1 is certain.
topic_clusters.level1_desc: Identifies outcomes as likely/unlikely. Finds probability of simple events (coin flip, die roll) with support. Cannot yet organize compound sample spaces.
topic_clusters.tier3_vocab: theoretical, experimental, compound event, tree diagram, simulation, complement, certain, impossible, likely, unlikely

topic_clusters.name: Proportional Relationships
topic_clusters.level4_desc: Represents proportional relationships algebraically (y = kx), graphically, and with tables. Determines and interprets the constant of proportionality in real-world contexts. Explains what points (x, y) on the graph mean, including (0, 0) and (1, r) where r is the unit rate.
topic_clusters.level3_desc: Decides whether two quantities are in a proportional relationship using tables, graphs, or equations. Computes unit rates including those with complex fractions. Identifies the constant of proportionality (k = y/x).
topic_clusters.level2_desc: Identifies proportional relationships in tables when the ratio is simple. Finds unit rates with whole numbers. Recognizes that proportional graphs pass through the origin.
topic_clusters.level1_desc: Recognizes that proportional means "constant ratio" but cannot consistently identify or compute k. Finds unit rates for simple whole-number ratios only.
topic_clusters.tier3_vocab: proportion, constant of proportionality, y = kx, equivalent ratio, cross multiply

topic_clusters.name: Scale Drawings & Similar Figures
topic_clusters.level4_desc: Reproduces a scale drawing at a different scale. Computes actual lengths and areas from scale drawings. Generalizes the critical attributes of similarity including ratios within and between similar shapes and applies to real-world design problems.
topic_clusters.level3_desc: Solves problems involving scale drawings of geometric figures, including computing actual lengths from the scale. Identifies corresponding sides and angles in similar figures. Uses ratios to determine if two shapes are similar.
topic_clusters.level2_desc: Uses a given scale factor to find one missing length. Identifies similar figures when told they are similar. Struggles to set up proportions independently.
topic_clusters.level1_desc: Understands that a scale drawing is a proportional representation but cannot independently find actual measurements or determine similarity.
topic_clusters.tier3_vocab: similar, proportion, enlarge, reduce, corresponding sides, corresponding angles

topic_clusters.name: Statistics & Sampling
topic_clusters.level4_desc: Uses data from a random sample to make valid inferences about a population. Assesses the validity of inferences by comparing samples. Informally assesses the degree of visual overlap of two numerical data distributions with similar variabilities and draws conclusions about population differences.
topic_clusters.level3_desc: Understands that statistics can be used to gain information about a population by examining a representative sample. Uses random samples to draw inferences. Compares two populations using measures of center and variability from random samples.
topic_clusters.level2_desc: Describes the difference between a sample and a population. Recognizes that random samples tend to be representative. Compares two data sets by looking at dot plots or box plots side by side.
topic_clusters.level1_desc: Recognizes that data comes from a group but does not distinguish sample from population. Reads comparative displays with support but cannot draw independent conclusions about populations.
topic_clusters.tier3_vocab: population, sample, random sample, inference, bias, variability, mean, median, MAD, comparative, dot plot, box plot

topic_clusters.name: Functions
topic_clusters.level4_desc: Compares properties of two functions represented in different ways (algebraically, graphically, numerically in tables, or by verbal descriptions). Constructs a function to model a linear relationship and determines rate of change and initial value. Describes qualitatively the functional relationship between two quantities by analyzing a graph (increasing/decreasing, linear/non-linear).
topic_clusters.level3_desc: Understands that a function assigns exactly one output to each input. Identifies functions from ordered pairs, tables, mappings, and graphs (vertical line test). Writes equations for linear functions in y = mx + b form. Gives examples of non-linear functions.
topic_clusters.level2_desc: Identifies whether a table or set of ordered pairs represents a function. Recognizes linear vs. non-linear functions on a graph. Writes the equation of a linear function with support.
topic_clusters.level1_desc: Understands the concept of input → output. Identifies functions in mapping diagrams. Cannot yet write function equations or distinguish linear from non-linear independently.
topic_clusters.tier3_vocab: function, input, output, domain, range, mapping, linear function, non-linear, rate of change, initial value

topic_clusters.name: Linear Equations & Slope
topic_clusters.level4_desc: Writes equations in y = mx + b form from tables, graphs, and descriptions. Solves linear equations with variables on both sides, including cases with no solution or infinitely many solutions. Explains why the slope is the same between any two distinct points on a line using similar triangles.
topic_clusters.level3_desc: Graphs proportional and non-proportional linear relationships. Determines slope and y-intercept from tables, graphs, and equations. Solves multi-step linear equations with rational coefficients. Distinguishes between proportional (y = kx) and non-proportional (y = mx + b, b ≠ 0) linear situations.
topic_clusters.level2_desc: Calculates slope from two points or a graph. Identifies slope and y-intercept from y = mx + b form. Solves two-step linear equations. Graphs lines from equations with support.
topic_clusters.level1_desc: Identifies slope as "steepness" on a graph. Recognizes y = mx + b as a line. Solves one-step equations. Needs step-by-step guidance to graph lines or calculate slope.
topic_clusters.tier3_vocab: y = mx + b, rate of change, coefficient, solution, substitution, elimination, equivalent equations

topic_clusters.name: Pythagorean Theorem
topic_clusters.level4_desc: Applies the Pythagorean theorem and its converse to solve problems in two and three dimensions. Determines the distance between two points on a coordinate plane. Explains a proof of the Pythagorean theorem using area models or algebraic reasoning.
topic_clusters.level3_desc: Applies a² + b² = c² to find unknown side lengths in right triangles. Uses the converse to determine if a triangle is a right triangle. Applies the theorem to find the distance between two points on a coordinate plane.
topic_clusters.level2_desc: Uses a² + b² = c² to find the hypotenuse when given both legs. Struggles to solve for a leg. Finds distance on a coordinate plane with step-by-step guidance.
topic_clusters.level1_desc: Identifies the hypotenuse as the longest side. Recognizes the formula a² + b² = c² but cannot yet apply it to solve for unknowns independently.
topic_clusters.tier3_vocab: right triangle, a² + b² = c², converse, distance, square root, diagonal

topic_clusters.name: Real Numbers & Irrational Numbers
topic_clusters.level4_desc: Classifies numbers as rational or irrational with justification. Approximates irrational numbers (including π and square roots of non-perfect squares) to any desired degree of accuracy. Locates irrational numbers precisely on a number line using successive approximation.
topic_clusters.level3_desc: Knows that numbers that are not rational are irrational. Understands that every number has a decimal expansion; rational numbers terminate or repeat, irrational numbers do neither. Approximates irrational numbers using rational approximations and locates them approximately on a number line.
topic_clusters.level2_desc: Identifies perfect squares and their roots. Estimates square roots of non-perfect squares between two whole numbers. Recognizes π as irrational. Struggles to place irrational numbers precisely on a number line.
topic_clusters.level1_desc: Knows some perfect squares (1, 4, 9, 16, 25). Recognizes that √2 is not a whole number. Cannot yet distinguish rational from irrational or approximate irrational numbers.
topic_clusters.tier3_vocab: rational, irrational, real number, square root, cube root, pi, non-repeating, non-terminating, number line

topic_clusters.name: Scatter Plots & Bivariate Data
topic_clusters.level4_desc: Constructs scatter plots, identifies clusters and outliers, and fits a line to data that suggests a linear association. Uses the equation of the linear model to make predictions and assess model fit. Distinguishes between linear and non-linear associations and between correlation and causation.
topic_clusters.level3_desc: Constructs and interprets scatter plots for bivariate data. Describes patterns (positive, negative, no association; linear, non-linear). Draws an informal trend line and uses it to make predictions. Interprets slope and y-intercept of a linear model in context.
topic_clusters.level2_desc: Plots bivariate data on a scatter plot. Identifies the overall trend as positive, negative, or no association. Draws a trend line with guidance but struggles to write its equation.
topic_clusters.level1_desc: Plots individual points on a scatter plot. Recognizes that scatter plots show two-variable relationships. Cannot yet identify trends or draw a trend line independently.
topic_clusters.tier3_vocab: scatter plot, trend line, line of best fit, positive association, negative association, no association, linear, non-linear, cluster, outlier, prediction

topic_clusters.name: Scientific Notation
topic_clusters.level4_desc: Performs operations (add, subtract, multiply, divide) with numbers in scientific notation and chooses appropriate units of measurement for very large or very small quantities. Interprets scientific notation in real-world contexts (astronomy, biology, chemistry). Compares numbers in scientific notation by reasoning about orders of magnitude.
topic_clusters.level3_desc: Converts between standard decimal notation and scientific notation. Interprets numbers expressed in scientific notation in context. Compares two numbers in scientific notation.
topic_clusters.level2_desc: Writes numbers in scientific notation when the coefficient is a single digit. Converts from scientific notation to standard form. Compares numbers in scientific notation when the exponents are the same.
topic_clusters.level1_desc: Recognizes scientific notation format (a × 10ⁿ). Converts small numbers in scientific notation to standard form with support. Cannot yet compare or compute with numbers in scientific notation.
topic_clusters.tier3_vocab: exponent, power of 10, coefficient, magnitude, very large, very small

topic_clusters.name: Slope & Rate of Change
topic_clusters.level4_desc: Uses similar right triangles to explain why slope is constant between any two points on a non-vertical line. Compares slopes from different representations (table, graph, equation, description). Interprets slope as rate of change in real-world contexts and explains what the sign and magnitude mean.
topic_clusters.level3_desc: Calculates slope from a graph, table, or two points using rise/run. Interprets the unit rate as the slope of the graph for proportional relationships. Identifies positive, negative, zero, and undefined slopes.
topic_clusters.level2_desc: Calculates slope from a graph by counting rise and run. Identifies positive vs. negative slope. Struggles to calculate slope from a table or two points without graphing first.
topic_clusters.level1_desc: Recognizes steeper vs. less steep lines visually. Understands slope relates to "how fast" something changes. Cannot yet compute slope from coordinates independently.
topic_clusters.tier3_vocab: rate of change, steepness, positive slope, negative slope, zero slope, undefined slope, similar triangles

topic_clusters.name: Systems of Equations
topic_clusters.level4_desc: Solves systems algebraically (substitution and elimination) and graphically. Identifies systems with one solution, no solution, or infinitely many solutions and explains the geometric meaning. Creates and solves systems from real-world contexts.
topic_clusters.level3_desc: Solves pairs of simultaneous linear equations by graphing and algebraically. Identifies the solution as the point of intersection. Verifies solutions by substituting back into both equations.
topic_clusters.level2_desc: Solves a system by graphing when both equations are in slope-intercept form. Identifies the intersection point as the solution. Uses substitution with support for simple systems.
topic_clusters.level1_desc: Understands that two lines can intersect. Graphs individual lines but cannot reliably identify or verify the intersection point as a solution to the system.
topic_clusters.tier3_vocab: intersection, substitution, elimination, no solution, infinitely many solutions, parallel, same line

topic_clusters.name: Transformations & Congruence
topic_clusters.level4_desc: Describes sequences of transformations that carry one figure onto another. Explains the effects of transformations on coordinates algebraically (e.g., (x,y) → (−x,y) for reflection over y-axis). Understands that congruence results from rigid motions and similarity results from rigid motions followed by dilations.
topic_clusters.level3_desc: Performs translations, rotations (90°, 180°, 270°), reflections, and dilations on coordinate planes. Identifies whether transformations preserve congruence. Determines if two figures are congruent or similar based on transformation sequences.
topic_clusters.level2_desc: Performs translations and reflections over the axes. Identifies congruent figures. Performs dilations with a given scale factor and center at origin. Struggles with rotations.
topic_clusters.level1_desc: Recognizes translations, reflections, and rotations visually. Identifies congruent vs. non-congruent figures. Cannot yet perform transformations on a coordinate plane independently.
topic_clusters.tier3_vocab: translation, rotation, reflection, dilation, congruent, similar, rigid motion, pre-image, image, orientation

topic_clusters.name: Volume — Cylinders, Cones & Spheres
topic_clusters.level4_desc: Solves real-world problems involving volumes of cones, cylinders, and spheres, including composite solids. Explains the relationship between the volumes of a cylinder, cone, and sphere with equal dimensions. Derives volume formulas through reasoning about cross-sections.
topic_clusters.level3_desc: Knows and applies the volume formulas for cones (V = ⅓πr²h), cylinders (V = πr²h), and spheres (V = ⁴⁄₃πr³). Solves word problems requiring volume calculations.
topic_clusters.level2_desc: Applies the cylinder volume formula correctly. Uses the cone and sphere formulas with support. Confuses which formula to apply in context.
topic_clusters.level1_desc: Identifies cylinders, cones, and spheres. Recognizes that volume formulas use π and radius. Cannot yet independently select or apply the correct formula.
topic_clusters.tier3_vocab: sphere, radius, height, π, V = πr²h, V = ⅓πr²h, V = ⁴⁄₃πr³, base area

topic_clusters.name: Data Analysis & Statistics
topic_clusters.level4_desc: Interprets and compares data displays. Fits linear models to scatter plots, interprets slope and intercept in context, and assesses model fit using residuals.
topic_clusters.level3_desc: Creates and interprets scatter plots. Fits a trend line and uses it for predictions. Calculates and interprets mean, median, IQR, and standard deviation.
topic_clusters.level2_desc: Creates scatter plots and identifies trend direction. Calculates mean and median. Reads and interprets box plots.
topic_clusters.level1_desc: Plots data points on a scatter plot. Calculates mean of a small data set. Cannot yet fit trend lines or interpret spread.
topic_clusters.tier3_vocab: residual, mean, median, IQR, standard deviation, outlier, line of best fit

topic_clusters.name: Exponential Functions
topic_clusters.level4_desc: Compares linear and exponential growth models. Interprets exponential functions in context (population, compound interest, decay). Writes exponential functions from tables and descriptions.
topic_clusters.level3_desc: Identifies exponential growth and decay from equations, tables, and graphs. Evaluates exponential expressions. Distinguishes between linear and exponential patterns.
topic_clusters.level2_desc: Evaluates exponential expressions for given inputs. Recognizes exponential growth from a table (constant ratio). Graphs simple exponential functions from a table.
topic_clusters.level1_desc: Understands that exponents mean repeated multiplication. Evaluates simple powers. Cannot yet distinguish exponential from linear growth.
topic_clusters.tier3_vocab: decay, base, exponent, growth factor, asymptote, compound interest

topic_clusters.name: Expressions & Polynomials
topic_clusters.level4_desc: Factors higher-degree polynomials and uses structure to simplify complex expressions. Connects polynomial operations to area models and real-world contexts fluently.
topic_clusters.level3_desc: Interprets parts of an expression (terms, factors, coefficients). Adds, subtracts, and multiplies polynomials. Factors quadratic trinomials and differences of squares.
topic_clusters.level2_desc: Combines like terms and distributes. Multiplies binomials using FOIL or area model. Factors simple trinomials with leading coefficient 1.
topic_clusters.level1_desc: Identifies terms and coefficients. Combines like terms with support. Cannot yet factor or multiply polynomials independently.
topic_clusters.tier3_vocab: polynomial, monomial, binomial, trinomial, factor, FOIL, distribute, like terms

topic_clusters.name: Linear Equations & Inequalities
topic_clusters.level4_desc: Solves multi-step equations and inequalities with variables on both sides, including absolute value. Creates equations from complex real-world contexts and interprets solutions.
topic_clusters.level3_desc: Solves two-step and multi-step linear equations and inequalities. Creates equations and inequalities from word problems. Graphs solution sets on a number line.
topic_clusters.level2_desc: Solves two-step equations. Solves simple inequalities and graphs on a number line. Writes equations from basic word problems with support.
topic_clusters.level1_desc: Solves one-step equations. Recognizes inequality symbols. Cannot yet set up equations from word problems independently.
topic_clusters.tier3_vocab: variable, solution, inverse operation, absolute value, compound inequality

topic_clusters.name: Linear Functions & Graphing
topic_clusters.level4_desc: Writes linear functions from any representation (table, graph, verbal, two points). Interprets slope and intercepts in context. Compares linear models and makes predictions.
topic_clusters.level3_desc: Graphs linear equations in slope-intercept and standard form. Identifies slope and y-intercept from equations and graphs. Writes equations from graphs and tables.
topic_clusters.level2_desc: Graphs lines from slope-intercept form. Calculates slope from two points. Identifies y-intercept from a graph.
topic_clusters.level1_desc: Plots points on a coordinate plane. Recognizes y = mx + b form. Cannot yet independently calculate slope or write equations.
topic_clusters.tier3_vocab: rate of change, slope-intercept form, standard form, point-slope form, domain, range

topic_clusters.name: Quadratic Functions
topic_clusters.level4_desc: Analyzes quadratic functions using vertex form, standard form, and factored form. Connects zeros, vertex, axis of symmetry, and transformations. Models real-world problems with quadratics.
topic_clusters.level3_desc: Graphs quadratic functions and identifies vertex, axis of symmetry, and zeros. Solves quadratic equations by factoring and using the quadratic formula.
topic_clusters.level2_desc: Graphs quadratics from a table of values. Identifies the vertex on a graph. Solves quadratic equations by factoring simple trinomials.
topic_clusters.level1_desc: Recognizes a parabola shape. Identifies that quadratics have an x-squared term. Cannot yet solve quadratic equations independently.
topic_clusters.tier3_vocab: axis of symmetry, zeros, roots, factored form, standard form, vertex form, quadratic formula, discriminant

topic_clusters.name: Systems of Equations & Inequalities
topic_clusters.level4_desc: Solves systems using substitution, elimination, and graphing. Chooses the most efficient method for a given system. Solves systems of inequalities and interprets solution regions.
topic_clusters.level3_desc: Solves systems of two linear equations by graphing and algebraically (substitution or elimination). Interprets the solution as a point of intersection.
topic_clusters.level2_desc: Solves systems by graphing when both equations are in slope-intercept form. Uses substitution with simple systems.
topic_clusters.level1_desc: Understands that two lines can intersect at a point. Graphs individual lines but cannot reliably find intersection.
topic_clusters.tier3_vocab: elimination, intersection, consistent, inconsistent, dependent, independent

topic_clusters.name: Exponential & Logarithmic Functions
topic_clusters.level4_desc: Solves exponential and logarithmic equations using properties of logarithms. Models real-world phenomena (half-life, pH, decibels, compound interest) with exponential and logarithmic functions.
topic_clusters.level3_desc: Evaluates logarithms. Converts between exponential and logarithmic forms. Uses properties of logarithms (product, quotient, power rules). Graphs exponential and logarithmic functions.
topic_clusters.level2_desc: Evaluates logarithms for powers of the base. Converts between exponential and logarithmic form. Graphs exponential functions with transformations.
